| Code  | Fixed Â§12 bug #6 in `R/calc_pti_helpers.R::get_scores_data`: singleton `(year, var_code)` groups now produce the neutral score `0` for non-`NA` values instead of `NA`. Root cause: `sd()` of a length-1 vector returns `NA` (not `NaN`), so the existing `ifelse(is.nan(value), 0, value)` filter missed singleton groups. Fix: precompute a per-row `.was_na` flag, run the existing standardise step, then patch `dplyr::if_else(dplyr::n() == 1L & !.was_na, 0, value)` before the `is.nan` rewrite. Input-`NA` rows are preserved as `NA` (via `.was_na`); the `.was_na` column is dropped before the function returns. First attempt used a single nested `dplyr::if_else` on `dplyr::n() == 1L` directly but dplyr's strict size-checking rejected the scalar condition with vector branches (`vec_check_size: true must have size 1, not size 27`); restructured to a length-`n` condition. Design choice (Interpretation X, surfaced at the gate): branch on literal group size, not on `sum(!is.na(value)) <= 1` â€” Y was deferred as a follow-up Â§12 row if a consumer needs "effectively n=1" coverage. NAMESPACE delta: added `dplyr::if_else` and `dplyr::n` `importFrom` lines (qualified-call convention). Caller audit: `R/mod_calc_pti2.R:81`, `R/run_pti_pipeline.R:64`, `R/fct_validate_metadata.R:59` are pipe links that don't inspect cardinalities; `tests/testthat/helper-test-data.R:39`'s downstream `mean ~= 0` assertion in `test-calc-pipeline.R:172` had an `is.na(means$m)` filter explicitly to dodge this bug â€” that filter is now a no-op, still safe. |

Renamed the `test_that` description to `"get_scores_data: 1-row groups produce 0 (no variance to scale)"` and replaced the `arch-03 Â§"Known Issues to Pin"` comment with a one-line rationale ("single observation has no variance to scale; treated as neutral score 0"). Suite stays at 682 PASS -- one expect_true swapped to one expect_equal, no count delta. |

Two-part rewrite at L48-58: (a) replaced `stringr::str_detect(names(wtd_scrd_dta), adm_from)` with `stringr::str_detect(names(wtd_scrd_dta), paste0("^", adm_from, "(_|$)"))` so iterating with `adm_from = "admin1"` no longer also picks up slots named `"admin10_..."`; (b) replaced the silent `length == 1` short-circuit (else branch returned `imap(adm_lvls, ~ NULL)`) with a loud `stop("more than one slot in 'wtd_scrd_dta' matches admin level '{adm_from}': {names}", call. = FALSE)` when >1 slots resolve to a single level. | Code  | Fixed Â§12 bug #10 in `R/mod_drop_inval_adm.R::get_vars_un_avbil` -- adopted contract X (strict no-extrapolation) from the design gate. Body shrank from ~25 lines (lex `arrange()` + triple `lag()` back-fill + operator-precedence typo `is.na(value & !any_larger)` parsing as `is.na((value & !any_larger))` + `lead()`-derived `any_larger` flag) to ~10 lines: `expand.grid` + `tibble::as_tibble` + `dplyr::anti_join` over the distinct `(var_code, admin_level)` set. Pre-fix asymmetry: an indicator with data only at admin1 was silently `lag()`-filled into admin2 / admin4 rows, so admin2 + admin4 were never surfaced as unavailable; reverse direction (admin2-only -> admin1) worked because admin1 had no `lag`. Post-fix both directions surface symmetrically. Decision rationale at the gate: X over Y (aggregate-up only) and Z (bidirectional) -- X matches the existing `weighting an unavailable var produces drops` test (`var_nval4_small_skewd_adm4` admin4-only -> admin1 + admin2 dropped) and avoids re-encoding extrapolation that `expand_adm_levels` already handles in the calc pipeline. NAMESPACE delta: dropped `dplyr::lead`, `dplyr::lag`, `dplyr::group_by`, `dplyr::count`, `dplyr::rename`, `dplyr::mutate`, `dplyr::filter`, `dplyr::left_join`, `dplyr::arrange` from the `@importFrom`; added `dplyr::distinct`, `dplyr::anti_join`, `tibble::as_tibble`. Dropped `"any_larger"` from `R/devPTIpack-package.R::globalVariables()` (column no longer in output; `grep` confirmed no caller in `R/` or `tests/` read it). Caller-graph audit: `get_min_admin_wght` only `pull(admin_level)` from the result, so dropping the `any_larger` column is safe. |

Tier-2 `test-mod-drop-inval-adm.R::"mod_drop_inval_adm: indicator missing at admin2 -> admin2 removed"` -- reverse-direction sibling of the existing `missing at admin1 -> admin1 removed` test, replacing the prior 8-line `# Note on symmetry. ... Not testing it here to avoid coupling this Tier-2 module test to a Tier-1 bug.` comment block. Two pre-existing tests updated to match the new contract: (a) `test-drop-inval-adm.R::"get_min_admin_wght: a fully-available var drops nothing"` -- swapped the picked var from `var_nval3_skewd_adm1` (admin1-only; the prior comment claimed it was "available at every admin level (cross-checked via probing)" but that probe was probing the broken function) to `var_nvalinf_unif_adm124`, the only fixture indicator with native data at every admin level; (b) `test-get_uavailab_admin.R::"get unavailable adming levels works"` -- bumped the asserted `nrow()` from 7 (lag-fill bug under-counted the admin1-only direction) to 12 (9 indicators x 3 admin levels - 15 cells with native data) with an inline rationale comment. | Code  | Fixed Â§12 bug #4 in `R/fct_validate_metadata.R::validate_read_shp` -- `str_c(character(0), collapse = "|")` returns `character(0)` (not `""`), so the downstream `str_detect(names(x), character(0))` errored with a vctrs `pattern must have size 1` when called on a "perfect" shapefile (every `admin{N}Pcod` field has a matching `admin{N}_*` slot, e.g. `ukr_shp`). The error was swallowed by the surrounding `testthat::test_that()` (registered as an `expectation_error` against the inner reporter), so externally the call returned silently while leaving the inner expectation in a failed state. Fix: compute `extra_level_vec` first (the diff between admin-code references and admin-slot names), then guard the diagnostic-label construction (`str_c collapse`, `keep`, `str_detect on names`) with `if (length(extra_level_vec) > 0)`; on empty extras, set both label strings to `""` and let the (already correct) `expect_true(all(... %in% ...))` pass cleanly. Caller-graph: only call site is `validate_metadata` at `R/fct_validate_metadata.R:35`. No NAMESPACE delta. Out of scope: the broader arch-01 refactor target -- converting the runtime `testthat::test_that` machinery into ordinary validation calls -- left for a future PR. |

## 2026-05-07

| Scope | Change |
| ----- | ------ |
| Code  | Fixed Â§12 bug #13 in `R/mod_dwnld_dta.R::mod_dwnld_file_server` + `R/launch_pti.R` under **Arch-2** (auto-materialize, raised by the user at the scope-proposal gate). Two coupled changes: **(1)** `mod_dwnld_file_server` validates `filepath` at registration (truthy character, length-1, exists on disk, not a directory). On invalid input calls `shinyjs::disable(outputId)` and the content callback writes an `unavailable-<date>.txt` placeholder if a click sneaks through anyway -- so the user never receives a silent broken `.html` again. On valid input the existing `basename(filepath)` filename builder is preserved. **(2)** New private helper `materialize_dwnld_paths(shp_dta, inp_dta, shapes_path, mtdtpdf_path, data_path)` writes in-memory `shp_dta` / `inp_dta` to session-scoped tempfiles under deliberately-distinct stems (`pti-shapes-<date>.rds`, `pti-data-export-<date>.xlsx`) when the corresponding paths are `NULL`; PDF stays `NULL` (no in-memory equivalent). Defaults on `launch_pti()` and `launch_pti_onepage()` flipped from `"."` to `NULL`. New `data_path` parameter on `launch_pti()` plumbs to `mod_dta_explorer2_server` -- closes a latent bug where the explorer's "Download data" button was *unreachable* from the public API. Iteration history: an interim attempt used `shinyjs::hide()` + `setdiff()`-ing `"metadata"` from `wt_dwnld_options` / `map_dwnld_options` to also clean up the orphaned " and ." connector text in the side-panel sentence; the connector logic lives in three side-panel renderers (PTI / compare / explorer) and the conditionals didn't reach all of them cleanly, so reverted to plain `disable()` + placeholder. Cosmetic " and ." sentence quirk remains when no PDF is supplied -- flagged as out-of-scope follow-up. NAMESPACE delta: added `importFrom(writexl, write_xlsx)`. Caller-graph: 5 `mod_dwnld_file_server` call sites untouched at the call site (paths now arrive valid post-materialization); 3 path-threading call sites in `launch_pti` updated. Manually verified by the user via `launch_pti(shp_dta = ukr_shp, inp_dta = ukr_mtdt_full)` -- shapes + data downloads work, metadata-PDF link is greyed out. |
| Tests | Added new Tier-1+Tier-2 test file `tests/testthat/test-mod-dwnld-file.R` (6 test_that blocks, 13 expectations). Two cover `materialize_dwnld_paths()`: NULL paths produce tempfile fallbacks under the documented stems (`^pti-shapes-.*\\.rds$`, `^pti-data-export-.*\\.xlsx$`); explicit paths are returned normalized. Four cover `mod_dwnld_file_server`: valid filepath does not call `shinyjs::disable`; NULL / "." / nonexistent paths each trigger exactly one `shinyjs::disable(id = outputId)` call. The `disable` mocks use `testthat::local_mocked_bindings(.package = "shinyjs")`; tests pin only the disable side-effect (the actual download streaming is a runtime concern verified manually). Suite goes 689 PASS -> 702 PASS (FAIL=0, SKIP=1) -- net +13 expectations from the new file. |
